text: I study empirical asset pricing, return predictability, factor models, and machine learning.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: My work centers on measurement: separating persistent information from transitory noise, reading risk exposures before they are fully priced, and keeping predictive models economically disciplined.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: Published and working papers are listed below.

text: Expected returns align more closely with factor risk once cross-factor covariances are estimated explicitly.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: The multifactor risk-return tradeoff turns strongly positive once factor covariances, not just variances, are accounted for.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: Covariances explain more variation in factor returns than variances and matter for both in-sample and out-of-sample prediction.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: Employee news consumption reveals macro risk exposures inside the firm before they fully surface in market outcomes.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: Employee news consumption helps characterize firms' exposure to macroeconomic risk.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: Firms whose employees read more macroeconomic news in advance are more exposed when conditions worsen later.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: Those firms also hedge more, face higher costs of capital, and invest and hire less.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: Separating persistent information from transitory noise sharpens characteristic-sorted strategies and pricing tests.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: Firm characteristics contain both persistent and transitory components, and the pricing of characteristics is driven largely by the persistent component.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: Strategies built on the persistent component deliver much higher Sharpe ratios than standard characteristic-based strategies.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: A large share of historically important commodity strategy premia appears to fade after financialization.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: Around four-fifths of commodity futures strategies that were profitable before financialization stop being profitable afterward.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: The decline appears to come from adverse repricing of a few systematically important factors.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: Value spreads forecast value returns across multiple asset classes, with a strong common component in risk premia.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: Value spreads predict value returns in equities, industries, commodities, currencies, and global bond and stock markets.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: A single common component explains about two-thirds of that predictability and aligns with broad variation in risk premia.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: Even high-Sharpe factor models do not span one another cleanly; strong fit alone does not resolve cross-model disagreement.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: Modern machine-learning factor models achieve high Sharpe ratios in very different ways and disagree on which factors are actually priced.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: Much of the return attributed to factor exposure by one model is treated as alpha by another.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: Economically structured neural networks generalize better than unconstrained prediction systems in asset-pricing settings.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: Unconstrained machine-learning models can predict short-run cross-sectional returns without generalizing to the time-series behavior of the market and long-short portfolios.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run

text: Imposing economically motivated structure restores that out-of-domain generalization.
source: local HTML DOM + provided desktop/mobile render artifacts visually inspected in this run
